page: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DLDSv5 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, featuring 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 614 GB of local disk storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute and memory workloads. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1899.08 and a multi-core score of 15528.25. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 102.63 GB/sec for read-only large blocks and 59.33 GB/sec for write large blocks. In text and data processing, the server completed bzip3 multi-threaded decompression at 46.61 GB/sec and registered 16.06 million operations per second in Redis SET operations. Notably, the server demonstrated strong performance in LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model, reaching 274.55 tokens per second, which places it in the top 25% tier for this specific metric.

The primary tradeoff of the D16lds_v5 is its low memory density relative to its compute capacity, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching. However, this resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-centric workloads that do not require massive RAM allocations. The inclusion of 614 GB of local storage provides a dedicated storage buffer for temporary files and local caching. This server is suited for workloads such as software compilation, web serving, lightweight text and image processing, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v5 is an x86_64 server instance within the DLDSv5 family, configured with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 614 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show average performance across standard compute and memory tests, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 9941.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 21155.48. It exhibits strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, reaching 274.55 tokens per second. Due to its low memory density, the instance represents a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand large memory footprints, such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v5 is a 16-vCPU server with an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 32 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, offering balanced compute performance with a low memory-to-core ratio.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v5 features 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, optimized for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements.
tagline: A compute-focused x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local storage, and a low memory-to-core ratio for cost-efficient processing.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
614 GB of local disk storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 274.55 tokens/sec
